server.description: Base URL of your Seerr instance (e.g. https://seer.example.com). The /api/v1 path is appended automatically.

api_key.description: Seerr API key. Generate one in Seerr → Settings → API Keys.

transport.description: Transport protocol for the MCP server.

addr.description: Bind address for the HTTP transport (host:port).

auth_token.description: Bearer token required by HTTP clients. Omit only when using --no-auth.

no_auth.description: Disable authentication entirely. Only use in trusted environments.

route_token.description: Secret path prefix for the MCP endpoint (e.g. 'abc123' → /abc123/mcp). Useful for clients that cannot send custom headers.

tls_cert.description: Path to TLS certificate file (PEM). Required together with tls_key to enable HTTPS.

tls_key.description: Path to TLS private key file (PEM). Required together with tls_cert to enable HTTPS.

cors.description: Enable CORS headers. Required for browser-based clients such as claude.ai.

multi_tenant.description: Route /{seer-api-token}/mcp so each user supplies their own API key in the URL.

log_file.description: Path to log file. For stdio transport this is the only way to capture logs.
